title: React Introduction and Setup
description: Learn the fundamentals of React, what it is, how it works, and how to set up a React development environment.
learningObjectives: Understand what React is and its purpose in web development
Set up a React development environment

subtopics.title: What is React?
subtopics.content:
React is a JavaScript library for building user interfaces, developed and maintained by Facebook.

**Key Points:**
- Component-based architecture
- Virtual DOM for efficient updates
- Declarative programming style
- Used for building single-page applications
- Large ecosystem and community

**Why Use React?**
- **Reusable Components**: Build encapsulated components that manage their own state
- **Virtual DOM**: Efficient updates without direct DOM manipulation
- **Declarative**: Describe what the UI should look like, React handles the rest
- **Ecosystem**: Huge library of components and tools
- **Performance**: Optimized rendering with virtual DOM

**Common Uses:**
- Single-page applications (SPAs)
- Mobile applications (React Native)
- Server-side rendering (Next.js)
- Desktop applications (Electron with React)

**React Version History:**
- React 0.3 (2013) - Initial release
- React 15 (2016) - Major improvements
- React 16 (2017) - Fiber architecture
- React 17 (2020) - No new features, focused on upgrades
- React 18 (2022) - Concurrent features, automatic batching

subtopics.title: Setting Up React
subtopics.content:
There are several ways to set up a React development environment.

**Using Create React App (CRA):**
```bash
npx create-react-app my-app
cd my-app
npm start
```

**Using Vite (Recommended):**
```bash
npm create vite@latest my-app -- --template react
cd my-app
npm install
npm run dev
```

**Using Next.js (Full-featured):**
```bash
npx create-next-app my-app
cd my-app
npm run dev
```

**Project Structure:**
```
my-app/
├── node_modules/
├── public/
│   └── index.html
├── src/
│   ├── App.css
│   ├── App.js
│   ├── index.css
│   └── index.js
├── package.json
└── README.md
```

**Best Practice:**
- Use Vite for new projects (faster, modern)
- Use Next.js for full-stack applications
- Use CRA for simple projects (being phased out)

glossary.definition: A JavaScript library for building user interfaces with a component-based architecture.
A reusable, self-contained piece of UI that can accept inputs (props) and return JSX.
A syntax extension for JavaScript that allows writing HTML-like code in JavaScript files.
A lightweight JavaScript representation of the real DOM that React uses for efficient updates.
A JavaScript function that returns JSX and can receive props as arguments.
A React feature that lets you group multiple elements without adding extra DOM nodes.
The process of converting JSX and modern JavaScript to browser-compatible JavaScript.
